slides.title: Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 7 English, Unit 5: Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection. Welcome to Unit 5! This unit takes you into the great outdoors. You will explore topics connected to outdoor adventures, nature and environmental protection. Whether it is hiking through a forest, camping beside a river, or working together to protect the natural world, this unit gives you the language skills to describe, discuss and write about these experiences with confidence. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use vocabulary related to outdoor equipment, adventure sports, nature features and eco-friendly practices
Read and understand texts about outdoor activities and environmental challenges
Use -ing forms (gerunds and present participles) correctly as subjects, objects and after prepositions
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use vocabulary related to outdoor equipment, adventure sports, nature features and eco-friendly practices. you will also read and understand texts about outdoor activities and environmental challenges. you will also use -ing forms (gerunds and present participles) correctly as subjects, objects and after prepositions. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: acknowledge, collaborate, demonstrate, endeavour, establish, evaluate. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection: source text 1
Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection: source text 1 and Outdoor Adventures and Environmental Protection: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: -ing Forms (Gerunds and Participles)
bullets: What it means and why it matters The -ing form of a verb is one of the most flexible tools in English. When we add -ing to a verb, the word can work in...
slides.narration: The first language focus is -ing Forms (Gerunds and Participles). What it means and why it matters The -ing form of a verb is one of the most flexible tools in English. When we add -ing to a verb, the word can work in different ways: as a noun (called a gerund), as part of a continuous tense, or as a describing word (called a present participle). In this lesson, we focus on using -ing forms as nouns (gerunds) — as the subject of a sentence, as the object after certain verbs, and after prepositions. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Speaking 1: Describe Your Ideal Outdoor Adventure
Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Describe Your Ideal Outdoor Adventure. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
